2026-04-09T04:56:26.8688772Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m

2026-04-09T04:56:26.8689472Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

2026-04-09T04:59:03.4285873Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T04:57:32.760608657Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57976,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document ResponseContext API, ItemExtensions, and resolveReferences in README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit a9a95cda (PR #57974) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57974 significantly refactored the input-access API:\\n- `ResponseContext.GetInputItemsAsync()` return type changed from `IReadOnlyList` to `IReadOnlyList` and gained a new `bool resolveReferences = true` parameter\\n- `ResponseContext.GetInputTextAsync()` was added as a new convenience method\\n- `ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable)` was added as a new **public** extension method\\n- `CreateResponse.GetInputText()` was made **internal** (was previously accessible; callers must migrate to `context.GetInputTextAsync()`)\\n\\nThe CHANGELOG and snippet files were updated, but the README Key Concepts section was not updated to document these new/changed APIs.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **No `ResponseContext` key concept section** вЂ” `ResponseContext` is one of the three parameters to `CreateAsync()` and is now the recommended entrypoint for all input access, but it has no dedicated subsection in the README. Users reading the README see it used in examples without understanding what it provides.\\n- **`resolveReferences` parameter is undocumented** вЂ” The new `bool resolveReferences = true` parameter for `GetInputItemsAsync()` is not explained anywhere in the README. Users need to know what passing `false` means (item references returned as `ItemReferenceParam` without resolution) and when to use it.\\n- **`ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable)` is not mentioned** вЂ” This new public static extension method is listed in the CHANGELOG but not in the README Key Concepts. Users who already have a list of `Item` objects (e.g., from a prior `GetInputItemsAsync()` call) should know they can call `.GetInputText()` directly without going through `ResponseContext`.\\n- **`CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded()` is missing from Key Concepts** вЂ” Also CHANGELOG-listed but absent from the README. Advanced users who need to access the raw (unresolved) expanded input as `OutputItem` types need to know this exists and how it differs from `context.GetInputItemsAsync()`.\\n- **`GetHistoryAsync()` is not mentioned in the README** вЂ” It is shown in `Sample5_ConversationHistory.md` but the README Key Concepts section has no mention of history access, even though it\\u0026#39;s a key multi-turn capability.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003eрџ“ђ Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` вЂ” Edit (add a new subsection and extend the existing Key Concepts)\\n\\nAdd a new `### ResponseContext` subsection **between** the existing `### ResponseHandler` subsection and `### ResponseEventStream`. Insert:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### ResponseContext\\n\\n`ResponseContext` is the second parameter to `CreateAsync`. It provides the handler with the response identifier, the resolved input items, conversation history, isolation keys, and forwarded client metadata.\\n\\n**Accessing input text (recommended for most handlers):**\\n\\n```\\n// Resolves input items (including item references) and returns all text as a single string.\\nvar inputText = await context.GetInputTextAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] individual input items:**\\n\\n```\\n// Returns IReadOnlyList. By default, item references are resolved to their Item subtypes.\\nvar items = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] Pass resolveReferences: false to skip reference resolution and preserve ItemReferenceParam objects.\\nvar rawItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(resolveReferences: false, cancellationToken=[REDACTED] text from an already-resolved item list (`ItemExtensions`):**\\n\\nIf you already have a list of `Item` objects (e.g., from a prior `GetInputItemsAsync()` call), you can extract text without a second async call:\\n\\n```\\n// Extension method on IEnumerable вЂ” no async needed.\\nstring text = items.GetInputText();\\n```\\n\\n**Accessing conversation history (multi-turn):**\\n\\n```\\n// Returns IReadOnlyList from previous turns (via previous_response_id or conversation context).\\nvar history = await context.GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] вЂ” raw input access (`CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded`):**\\n\\nWhen you need the raw, unresolved input as `OutputItem` types (before provider resolution), use the extension directly on the request:\\n\\n```\\n// Returns a List from the raw request body. Item references remain as ItemReferenceParam.\\nvar rawInput = request.GetInputExpanded();\\n```\\n\\nUse `context.GetInputItemsAsync()` in preference to `GetInputExpanded()` for normal handlers вЂ” it handles reference resolution and caching automatically.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nFor each snippet placeholder added in Step 1, write the corresponding snippet in a test file.\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs` вЂ” Edit (add new test methods)\\n\\nAdd after the existing `Implement_EchoHandler` test method block:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\npublic async Task ResponseContext_GetInputText()\\n{\\n    var context = new ResponseContext(\\u0026#34;test-id\\u0026#34;);\\n    #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetInputText\\n    var inputText = await context.GetInputTextAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED]    #endregion\\n    Assert.That(inputText, Is.Not.Null);\\n}\\n\\n[Test]\\npublic async Task ResponseContext_GetInputItems()\\n{\\n    var context = new ResponseContext(\\u0026#34;test-id\\u0026#34;);\\n    #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetInputItems\\n    var items = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED]    var rawItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(resolveReferences: false, cancellationToken=[REDACTED]    #endregion\\n    Assert.That(items, Is.Not.Null);\\n    Assert.That(rawItems, Is.Not.Null);\\n}\\n\\n[Test]\\npublic void ItemExtensions_GetInputText()\\n{\\n    var items = new List();\\n    #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ItemExtensionsGetInputText\\n    string text = items.GetInputText();\\n    #endregion\\n    Assert.That(text, Is.Not.Null);\\n}\\n\\n[Test]\\npublic async Task ResponseContext_GetHistory()\\n{\\n    var context = new ResponseContext(\\u0026#34;test-id\\u0026#34;);\\n    #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetHistory\\n    var history = await context.GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED]    #endregion\\n    Assert.That(history, Is.Not.Null);\\n}\\n```\\n\\nFor `Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetInputExpanded`, add a thin test using `CreateResponseExtensions.GetInputExpanded()` with a constructed `CreateResponse` instance (the snippet only requires it to compile).\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections. 2026-04-09T04:59:03.4349948Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T04:57:32.933661163Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57976,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document ResponseContext API, ItemExtensions, and resolveReferences in README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit a9a95cda (PR #57974) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57974 significantly refactored the input-access API:\\n- `ResponseContext.GetInputItemsAsync()` return type changed from `IReadOnlyList` to `IReadOnlyList` and gained a new `bool resolveReferences = true` parameter\\n- `ResponseContext.GetInputTextAsync()` was added as a new convenience method\\n- `ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable)` was added as a new **public** extension method\\n- `CreateResponse.GetInputText()` was made **internal** (was previously accessible; callers must migrate to `context.GetInputTextAsync()`)\\n\\nThe CHANGELOG and snippet files were updated, but the README Key Concepts section was not updated to document these new/changed APIs.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **No `ResponseContext` key concept section** вЂ” `ResponseContext` is one of the three parameters to `CreateAsync()` and is now the recommended entrypoint for all input access, but it has no dedicated subsection in the README. Users reading the README see it used in examples without understanding what it provides.\\n- **`resolveReferences` parameter is undocumented** вЂ” The new `bool resolveReferences = true` parameter for `GetInputItemsAsync()` is not explained anywhere in the README. Users need to know what passing `false` means (item references returned as `ItemReferenceParam` without resolution) and when to use it.\\n- **`ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable)` is not mentioned** вЂ” This new public static extension method is listed in the CHANGELOG but not in the README Key Concepts. Users who already have a list of `Item` objects (e.g., from a prior `GetInputItemsAsync()` call) should know they can call `.GetInputText()` directly without going through `ResponseContext`.\\n- **`CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded()` is missing from Key Concepts** вЂ” Also CHANGELOG-listed but absent from the README. Advanced users who need to access the raw (unresolved) expanded input as `OutputItem` types need to know this exists and how it differs from `context.GetInputItemsAsync()`.\\n- **`GetHistoryAsync()` is not mentioned in the README** вЂ” It is shown in `Sample5_ConversationHistory.md` but the README Key Concepts section has no mention of history access, even though it\\u0026#39;s a key multi-turn capability.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003eрџ“ђ Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` вЂ” Edit (add a new subsection and extend the existing Key Concepts)\\n\\nAdd a new `### ResponseContext` subsection **between** the existing `### ResponseHandler` subsection and `### ResponseEventStream`. Insert:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### ResponseContext\\n\\n`ResponseContext` is the second parameter to `CreateAsync`. It provides the handler with the response identifier, the resolved input items, conversation history, isolation keys, and forwarded client metadata.\\n\\n**Accessing input text (recommended for most handlers):**\\n\\n```\\n// Resolves input items (including item references) and returns all text as a single string.\\nvar inputText = await context.GetInputTextAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] individual input items:**\\n\\n```\\n// Returns IReadOnlyList. By default, item references are resolved to their Item subtypes.\\nvar items = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] Pass resolveReferences: false to skip reference resolution and preserve ItemReferenceParam objects.\\nvar rawItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(resolveReferences: false, cancellationToken=[REDACTED] text from an already-resolved item list (`ItemExtensions`):**\\n\\nIf you already have a list of `Item` objects (e.g., from a prior `GetInputItemsAsync()` call), you can extract text without a second async call:\\n\\n```\\n// Extension method on IEnumerable вЂ” no async needed.\\nstring text = items.GetInputText();\\n```\\n\\n**Accessing conversation history (multi-turn):**\\n\\n```\\n// Returns IReadOnlyList from previous turns (via previous_response_id or conversation context).\\nvar history = await context.GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] вЂ” raw input access (`CreateResponse.GetInputExpanded`):**\\n\\nWhen you need the raw, unresolved input as `OutputItem` types (before provider resolution), use the extension directly on the request:\\n\\n```\\n// Returns a List from the raw request body. Item references remain as ItemReferenceParam.\\nvar rawInput = request.GetInputExpanded();\\n```\\n\\nUse `context.GetInputItemsAsync()` in preference to `GetInputExpanded()` for normal handlers вЂ” it handles reference resolution and caching automatically.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nFor each snippet placeholder added in Step 1, write the corresponding snippet in a test file.\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs` вЂ” Edit (add new test methods)\\n\\nAdd after the existing `Implement_EchoHandler` test method block:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\npublic async Task ResponseContext_GetInputText()\\n{\\n    var context = new ResponseContext(\\u0026#34;test-id\\u0026#34;);\\n    #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetInputText\\n    var inputText = await context.GetInputTextAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED]    #endregion\\n    Assert.That(inputText, Is.Not.Null);\\n}\\n\\n[Test]\\npublic async Task ResponseContext_GetInputItems()\\n{\\n    var context = new ResponseContext(\\u0026#34;test-id\\u0026#34;);\\n    #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetInputItems\\n    var items = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED]    var rawItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(resolveReferences: false, cancellationToken=[REDACTED]    #endregion\\n    Assert.That(items, Is.Not.Null);\\n    Assert.That(rawItems, Is.Not.Null);\\n}\\n\\n[Test]\\npublic void ItemExtensions_GetInputText()\\n{\\n    var items = new List();\\n    #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ItemExtensionsGetInputText\\n    string text = items.GetInputText();\\n    #endregion\\n    Assert.That(text, Is.Not.Null);\\n}\\n\\n[Test]\\npublic async Task ResponseContext_GetHistory()\\n{\\n    var context = new ResponseContext(\\u0026#34;test-id\\u0026#34;);\\n    #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_GetHistory\\n    var history = await context.GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED]    #endregion\\n    Assert.That(history, Is.Not.Null);\\n}\\n```\\n\\nFor Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.\\n2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.\\n3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.\\n4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.\\n\\n## Minimal observed output\\n```text\\nassistant(stream)\\u003e \\u003cfull answer omitted\\u003e\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent\\nUnhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel\\u003cAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall\\u003e.Write(...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\n```\\n\\n## Suspected root cause\\nThe generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.\\n\\nRelevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`\\n\\n## Impact\\n- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.\\n- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.\\n- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.\\n\\n## Workaround currently used\\nApplication-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.\\n\\n## Request\\nPlease confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute generation.\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"brandonh-msft\",\"id\":20270743,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjcwNzQz\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20270743?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/brandonh-msft\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1069434707,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Service%20Attention\",\"name\":\"Service
